title: Coincidences in My Life Have Me Wondering
gist: Science writer George Musser recounts uncanny repeat encounters with the same backpacker across Africa and Hawaii, using them to reflect that even a committed materialist can find it rational to wonder whether there is hidden structure beyond 'atoms and void.'

insights.claim: Musser frames himself as a firm materialist who has never needed supernatural forces, holding that 'a few simple rules and a few basic ingredients beget a sublime diversity.'
insights.speaker: George Musser
insights.so_what: Personal-essay framing of a worldview; no market read.
insights.quote: To me, the magic of the universe is that it needs no magic.
insights.locator: I am a firm believer in materialism.

insights.claim: Musser concludes it is not irrational to wonder whether there is more to the world than atoms and void, despite his materialism, prompted by repeated astronomically unlikely encounters.
insights.speaker: George Musser
insights.so_what: Reflective epistemics on probability and meaning; no market read.
insights.quote: it is not irrational to wonder whether there is more to this world than atoms and void
insights.locator: episodes such as my serendipitous encounters with Chris
